payload.summary: Planned the production wiring for autopilot per-phase sub-agent dispatch. Approach A (all-phase wiring + D-1 + D-2 in one change) approved at Gate 1. Four work packages decomposed: wp-contracts (schemas + SQL migration), wp-skills-autopilot (helpers + runner + SKILL.md rewrite), wp-coordinator-status-discovery (status reporter + AgentInfo + DB migration apply), wp-integration (e2e + audit-log + runtime sync + docs).

decisions.title: SKILL.md prose dispatches harness Agent(...) tool
decisions.rationale: Matches the established repo convention; user explicitly chose the SKILL.md prompt-layer path at discovery. Avoids introducing a second parallel-execution pattern.

decisions.title:
system_prompt is folded into prompt text with 

---

 separator
decisions.rationale: Harness Agent(...) tool surface in this codebase exposes prompt, model, isolation, run_in_background — no system_prompt parameter. Folding preserves archetype semantics without harness changes.

decisions.title: build_phase_dispatch_kwargs returns a dict; orchestrator unpacks into Agent()
decisions.rationale: SKILL.md prose can't hold Python state across tool calls. Using runner.py CLI as the prose-Python boundary keeps each layer doing what it does best.

decisions.title: Inline fallback when archetype unresolvable or Agent() unavailable
decisions.rationale: User chose 'inline fallback' at discovery. Backward-compatible: operators on minimal harnesses still get an autopilot run, just with phase_archetype=null for affected phases.

decisions.title: Dedicated phase_archetype TEXT column on agent_sessions, not JSONB key
decisions.rationale: Field is queryable for filtering ('show all agents in reviewer archetype'); JSONB key access is awkward in SQL. Migrations are first-class in this repo, so adding one column is cheap.

decisions.title: INIT and SUBMIT_PR record archetype via state-only resolver, not run_phase_subagent
decisions.rationale: INIT is state-only by design D13 (archived). Routing it through run_phase_subagent would dispatch a sub-agent that does nothing. The state-only helper closes the spec scenario hole instead.

next_steps: wp-contracts: author the SQL migration, JSON schema, and OpenAPI delta first (no dependencies).
Then dispatch wp-skills-autopilot and wp-coordinator-status-discovery in parallel — they have non-overlapping write_allow scopes.
wp-integration runs last and depends on both implementation packages.
After PR merge, run the audit-log validator manually against a real /autopilot run for the rollout cross-check.

relevant_files.description: Design with 8 decisions, architecture diagram, risks
MODIFIED + ADDED requirements for production-path dispatch + helpers
MODIFIED + ADDED requirements for status reporter + AgentInfo persistence
SQL migration adding phase_archetype TEXT column
JSON Schema for the extended POST /status/report payload
